summary: The q2 dataset contains 10 problems (q1-q10), each showing exactly 100 issues, indicating uniform activity levels across the board with no single problem standing out by raw count. The provided context lacks differentiating metadata (issue types, resolution status, timestamps), so traction and solvability cannot be reliably distinguished from the available data alone. Additional metadata is required to produce actionable per-problem prioritization.
problems: All 10 problems (q1-q10) report an identical count of 100 issues, suggesting either a data cap, a placeholder value, or a logging artifact rather than genuine differential activity
No issue-level metadata (severity, category, status, age) is available, preventing identification of common patterns or root causes
Proof status is reported only as raw issue counts with no open-vs-resolved breakdown, so solvability and traction cannot be assessed
The uniform 100-count across every problem strongly indicates a truncation or default value, undermining confidence in the raw figures
highlights: Complete coverage: all 10 problems in the dataset are being tracked and reporting consistently
Uniform instrumentation suggests the issue-logging pipeline is operating across every problem without gaps
The dataset is structurally complete and ready for richer analysis once detailed metadata is attached

suggested_todos.title: Investigate the uniform 100-issue count to determine if it reflects a data cap, truncation, or placeholder
Enrich the dataset with issue-level metadata (status, severity, category, timestamp) to enable real prioritization
Add an open-vs-resolved breakdown to the proof status to surface near-solvable problems
Define traction metrics (recent activity, contributor count) to distinguish high-momentum problems
Re-run this insight report once differentiated data is available
